title: Binary search is sound and complete on a sorted array
statement_informal: Binary search over a monotone non-decreasing array a on index window [lo,hi) reports the key present iff some index in the window holds it. Soundness needs nothing; completeness uses monotonicity to keep the key in the recursed half. bsearch_correct: monotone a -> hi-lo<=fuel -> (bsearch ... = true <-> exists i, lo<=i<hi /\ a i = key).

cost_model.notes: Halves the window each step (~lg(hi-lo) comparisons). Soundness: a true verdict exhibits a matching index. Completeness: monotonicity forces a present key into the half searched (a mid < key => index > mid, etc).

references: Binary search (TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.2.1). Correctness (soundness + completeness) over a monotone array; the comparison count is work-units/binary-search-comparisons.
